QF.id: XIII Introduction to the Underlying Markets_69

question.text: Which of the following correctly ranks the three underlying markets by typical daily turnover in India (highest to lowest)?

answer.text: Forex market > Government securities market > Equity cash market
Equity cash market > Forex market > Government securities market
Government securities market > Equity cash market > Forex market
All three markets have approximately equal daily turnover

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Indian forex market has the highest daily turnover (interbank OTC forex is around USD 30-35 billion daily). The G-Sec market trades around Rs 30,000-50,000 crore daily. The equity cash market turnover is around Rs 60,000-80,000 crore daily but forex still exceeds both in absolute terms. **The Trap:** Many assume equity is the largest market because it gets the most media attention. Globally and in India, forex consistently has the highest turnover. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I covers forex market size. Series IV covers bond market depth. Series VIII and VII cover equity market structure. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The forex market's dominance in turnover means it offers the deepest liquidity — currency derivatives benefit from this liquidity, often having tighter bid-ask spreads than equivalent equity derivatives.
